evidence_label: assay-source refresh for recipient evidence card gate, not treatment advice
source: NCBI PubMed ESearch and ESummary plus current web search
current_query: (thalassemia OR beta-thalassemia) AND (HUDEP2 OR CD34 OR erythroid OR HbF OR HBG OR hemolysis OR heme assay OR reporter)

records.role: HUDEP2-linked endogenous HbF reporter context; supports asking whether a recipient can measure HbF or HBG without patient data
records.title: A cellular reporter system to evaluate endogenous fetal hemoglobin induction and screen for therapeutic compounds.

records.role: small-molecule HbF screening context; supports model, control, and raw-data capability questions
records.title: Identification of small molecule agonists of fetal hemoglobin expression for the treatment of sickle cell disease.

records.role: CD34+ synthetic beta-thalassemia erythropoiesis model; supports recipient model-evidence requirements
records.title: A synthetic model of human beta-thalassemia erythropoiesis using CD34+ cells from healthy adult donors.

records.role: flow cytometry HbF context; supports explicit HbF or F-cell measurement capability
records.title: Flow cytometric analysis of fetal hemoglobin in erythroid precursors of beta-thalassemia.

records.role: hemolysis cytotoxicity assay context; supports red-cell safety capability questions
records.title: Optimization of the Hemolysis Assay for the Assessment of Cytotoxicity.

records.role: current PubMed heme-assay record; optional hemolysis/severity context only
records.title: A Novel Plasma Heme Assay Reveals Disease Severity in Beta-Thalassemia and Sickle Cell Anemia.

decision_use: A recipient evidence card should require public source URLs, model capability, HbF or HBG measurement, maturation or viability, hemolysis or heme safety, raw-data export, cost and timeline labels, and ethics or biosafety constraints. No real recipient is selected or contacted.
